verdict_reasoning: BAX is a pro-apoptotic BCL-2 family member that is exclusively cytosolic at baseline with translocation to the mitochondrial outer membrane (MOM) during apoptosis. It has no transmembrane domain, no GPI anchor, and no outer-leaflet lipidation. It does not participate in documented PM cycling, dual localization, lysosomal exocytosis, cancer-cell ecto-display, or cell-death-induced surface externalization — its pro-apoptotic role is intramitochondrial pore formation. No cell-surface-directed therapeutic targets BAX on the plasma membrane. No documented PM pool in any cell state, tissue, or developmental context.
